message.content:
@startuml
actor Teams
participant "API" as API #D3D3D3
control BuildService #E6F2FF
control CommandParser #FFFACD
control BuildCommand #FFE4B5
boundary BuildSystem #98FB98
control BuildFactory #DDA0DD
database BuildDb #ADD8E6
control BuildMonitor #FAEBD7
entity NotificationService #AFEEEE
database NotificationDb #DEB887

Teams -> API : /api/messages
activate API
    API -> BuildService : ProcessCommandAsync()
        activate BuildService
        
            BuildService -> CommandParser : ParseCommand(command)
                activate CommandParser
                
                    alt command == 'build' 
                        return new BuildCommand(projectName, branch);
                        
                            note right of CommandParser\n#lightgreen:green background:#white:black text:\nBuildCommand { \n  projectName = string; \n  branch = string;\n}
                            
                    else invalid_command_type_error
                        
                    end
                    
                
                deactivate CommandParser
            
            
            opt if buildcommand != null then 
            
                BuildService->BuildCommand: Execute();
                    
                    activate BuildCommand
                    
                        BuildCommand --> BuildSystem : CreateBuildAsync(buildRequestDto);\n// DTO contains project name & git ref to use for this specific run.\n // Also includes user who triggered it so we can notify them later via email/slack etc.. once done successfully or failed due some reason like compilation errors during execution phase itself which would require manual intervention from developer side before proceeding further ahead steps below mentioned hereunder:-\nawait Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(1));   /* Simulate delay */return true ;     } catch(Exception ex){Console.WriteLine(ex.Message);}finally{this.Dispose ();}}}'/>


@enduml
